requirement: Can't view work history viaG UI or CLI. Check work is being recorded with correct thread id etc. and is being saved to permanent storage.

tasks.description: I need to understand the codebase first. Let me explore the project.

feature_slices.description: Implement thread-context propagation using context variables to ensure consistent thread-ID per work-entry.
acceptance: Unique thread ID is generated and propagated through all work-entry logs.
Thread-ID consistency is verified through parallel work-entry processing.

feature_slices.description: Extend the `WorkEntry` model in SQLAlchemy to include a `thread_id` field and enforce its uniqueness with a composite constraint.
acceptance: `WorkEntry` model includes `thread_id` with non-null constraint and unique composite constraint (with timestamp).
Existing migration strategy is defined for schema update.

feature_slices.description: Update `AuditService` to use the new thread context to persist work entries and handle retries for transient write failures.
acceptance: Audit service uses context-propagated `thread_id` for each `record_action` call.
Unit tests verify successful upserts and idempotency with duplicate `record_action` calls.

feature_slices.description: Add a `spine audit` CLI command that queries the GitHub audit repository and returns work history filtered by `thread-id`.
acceptance: CLI command `spine audit --thread-id <id>` fetches work history for a specific thread.
Test cases verify output format and filtering by `thread-id` without UI/CLI.

feature_slices.description: Expose a `/audit` REST endpoint for querying work history entries by `thread-id`, `timestamp`, and `action`.
acceptance: HTTP endpoint `/audit` responds with appropriate HTTP status codes and pagination for large datasets.
Tests confirm access control and error handling for invalid parameters.

feature_slices.description: Write unit tests and integration tests to verify thread-ID consistency, durability, and idempotency of work history entries.
acceptance: Unit tests confirm thread-ID propagation and unique constraint compliance.
Integration tests verify write persistence through process restart and handling of duplicate entries.
